audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in account_usability.c are guarded: `ber` is null-checked after `ber_init` (L68-71) before use; optional out-params `usabilityp` and `availablep` are null-guarded (L78, L107, L113); the only caller `tool_print_ctrls` (common.c:L2729) dispatches `ctrls[i]` under a loop invariant `ctrls[i] != NULL`, so `ctrl` is never NULL at the call site. Attacker-controlled BER data affects only the parsed values, not pointer validity.
